topic: Firmware Improvement Review — Ender 3 Pro "Hoverfly" (SKR Mini E3 V2.0, Sprite Pro Direct Drive, CRTouch)
summary: The firmware is in good overall shape, but two issues stand out as genuinely impactful. (1) THE BIGGEST GAP: LIN_ADVANCE is enabled but ADVANCE_K is 0.0, so the most valuable direct-drive feature is doing nothing. For the Sprite Pro direct drive a starting K of ~0.04 is correct, tuned to roughly 0.02-0.06 with PLA. (2) A LATENT CONFLICT: LIN_ADVANCE and S_CURVE_ACCELERATION are BOTH enabled. Marlin's own sanity check forbids this combination unless you also define EXPERIMENTAL_SCURVE; the pairing causes over/under-extrusion at accel/decel edges. Since the config currently compiles, either EXPERIMENTAL_SCURVE is implicitly set or this would fail to build — it must be resolved. The clean fix is to DISABLE S_CURVE_ACCELERATION (it has no benefit alongside LIN_ADVANCE, conflicts with Junction Deviation too, and is mutually exclusive with Input Shaping). Input Shaping is feasible on the SKR Mini E3 V2.0 (STM32F103 @ 72 MHz) but the F103 is the weakest 32-bit board for it — keep step rates modest (this Ender's ~80 steps/mm and ~500 mm/s^2 accel are well within limits) and you MUST disable S_CURVE_ACCELERATION first (they are incompatible). The README "Dual Z" claim conflicts with firmware: Z2_DRIVER_TYPE is commented out, so firmware is single-Z. The SKR Mini E3 V2.0 has NO spare stepper driver (X/Y/Z/E only), so true independent dual-Z (with Z auto-alignment) is hardware-impossible on this board — any second Z motor must be wired in series/parallel to the single Z driver, which is mechanically fine but cannot self-level the gantry. Bed PID, junction deviation, GRID density, sensorless homing, probe refinements, EDITABLE_STEPS, and thermal protection are addressed individually below. Tune order: PID (hotend+bed) -> Junction Deviation -> disable S_CURVE -> Input Shaping -> LIN_ADVANCE K (LA must be tuned LAST, after IS).

recommendations.title: Tune LIN_ADVANCE ADVANCE_K for the Sprite Pro direct drive (biggest single win)
recommendations.currentValue: ADVANCE_K 0.0 (LIN_ADVANCE enabled but untuned = effectively off)
recommendations.recommendedValue: Start ADVANCE_K 0.04; tune to ~0.02-0.06 for PLA (per-filament)
recommendations.rationale: LIN_ADVANCE compensates for pressure buildup in the melt zone so corners/line-starts get correct width. With K=0.0 the feature does nothing. Direct-drive extruders need small K values (short filament path = low 'spring'). The Sprite Pro is a direct drive; community Klipper pressure-advance values for the Sprite Pro land around 0.037-0.047 s (Polymaker/Overture PLA), which maps closely to Marlin K ~0.03-0.05. Ellis recommends finding a rough range then refining in 0.002 steps. K must be tuned AFTER Input Shaping, because IS changes motion timing. Do NOT trust a single value across spools — re-check per filament/temp.
recommendations.howToApply: Set in Configuration_adv.h: #define ADVANCE_K 0.04 . For live tuning use M900 K0.04 (then M500 to save). Calibrate with Ellis' Pattern/Tower method or Marlin's K-factor test: print a single-wall pattern sweeping K via M900, pick the K with uniform line width and sharpest corners (low K = corner blobs, high K = corner gaps).

recommendations.title: Resolve LIN_ADVANCE + S_CURVE_ACCELERATION conflict by disabling S_CURVE
recommendations.currentValue: S_CURVE_ACCELERATION ENABLED and LIN_ADVANCE ENABLED simultaneously
recommendations.rationale: Marlin has a sanity check that errors out when LIN_ADVANCE and S_CURVE_ACCELERATION are both enabled unless EXPERIMENTAL_SCURVE is explicitly defined — because the combination is known-buggy: S-curve's Bezier accel profile breaks LIN_ADVANCE's timing prediction, producing over/under-extrusion at accel/decel edges (worst at high speed + low accel). S_CURVE also conflicts with JUNCTION_DEVIATION (S-curve effectively stops working unless CLASSIC_JERK is used). And S_CURVE is mutually exclusive with Input Shaping. Since this config currently builds, EXPERIMENTAL_SCURVE is likely silently in play or the build is masking the warning — either way the safe, recommended path is to drop S_CURVE entirely. You lose nothing meaningful: with LIN_ADVANCE + Junction Deviation (and later Input Shaping) doing the smoothing, S_CURVE is redundant and harmful here.
recommendations.howToApply: In Configuration.h comment out: //#define S_CURVE_ACCELERATION . Rebuild and reflash. Do NOT use EXPERIMENTAL_SCURVE as a workaround.

recommendations.title: Enable Input Shaping (X/Y) — feasible on SKR Mini E3 V2.0 with caveats
recommendations.recommendedValue: Enable both; set frequencies via M593 after measuring (typical Ender 3 X/Y resonance ~ 35-60 Hz)
recommendations.rationale: Input Shaping removes ringing/ghosting and lets you raise accel without artifacts. The SKR Mini E3 V2.0 uses an STM32F103 @ 72 MHz (Cortex-M3) — the weakest 32-bit MCU that supports Marlin IS. IS adds significant per-step CPU load; the F103 can stall/grind at very high combined step rates. This printer is low-risk: ~80 steps/mm on X/Y and modest accel (500 mm/s^2) keep step rates far below the limit, so IS is feasible. REQUIREMENT: S_CURVE_ACCELERATION must be disabled (incompatible). For tuning IS, set high test accel (>=1000 mm/s^2) so ringing is visible. IS must be calibrated BEFORE LIN_ADVANCE K. Watch for the known MULTISTEPPING_LIMIT grinding bug at high speeds and the 'IS over-compensates at low speeds' behavior.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration_adv.h: uncomment #define INPUT_SHAPING_X and #define INPUT_SHAPING_Y. Print Marlin/TH3D ringing tower (15-60 Hz sweep). Measure the cleanest band, compute freq (or use TH3D calculator), then M593 X F<freqX> Y F<freqY> and M500 to save. Default damping zeta 0.15 is a good start. Re-tune LIN_ADVANCE afterward.

recommendations.title: Resolve Dual-Z discrepancy: README says dual-Z, firmware is single-Z
recommendations.currentValue: Z2_DRIVER_TYPE COMMENTED OUT (firmware single-Z); README lists 'Dual Z-axis' mod
recommendations.recommendedValue: Keep single-Z in firmware (correct for this board); document that any 2nd Z motor is wired to the single Z driver
recommendations.rationale: Independent dual-Z (separate Z2 driver enabling Z_STEPPER_AUTO_ALIGN / G34 gantry self-leveling) requires a 5th stepper driver. The SKR Mini E3 V2.0 has exactly four integrated TMC2209s (X/Y/Z/E) and NO spare driver port — so true independent dual-Z is hardware-impossible on this board. Therefore Z2_DRIVER_TYPE being commented out is CORRECT. If a second Z motor is physically installed, it must be wired in series (or parallel) to the single Z driver via a splitter; both motors then move identically off one driver. That is mechanically valid and improves gantry rigidity, but it CANNOT auto-align/level the two sides — manual squaring only. The README should be corrected to say 'dual Z motors driven from a single driver (series), no independent Z2 / no G34', to avoid implying gantry auto-leveling exists.
recommendations.howToApply: No firmware change needed. Leave //#define Z2_DRIVER_TYPE commented. If running two motors on one driver, ensure the Z driver current (M906 Z / Configuration_adv TMC current) accounts for the added load; verify both wired in series (not over-current). Update README mod description. (Upgrading to a board with a spare driver would be required for real independent dual-Z.)

recommendations.title: Enable bed PID (PIDTEMPBED) and autotune
recommendations.recommendedValue: Enable PIDTEMPBED; run M303 E-1 to get Kp/Ki/Kd
recommendations.rationale: Bang-bang switches the bed fully on/off, causing slow oscillation around the setpoint (often +/-1-3 C). PID feathers power for a tighter, more stable bed temperature, which improves first-layer consistency and reduces thermal drift mid-print. The SKR Mini E3 V2.0 drives the bed via MOSFET that supports PWM, so PID is safe here. This is a clear quality/stability upgrade with no downside for this hardware.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration.h: uncomment #define PIDTEMPBED. Reflash, then run M303 E-1 S60 C8 U1 (autotune at 60 C, 8 cycles, auto-apply), then M500. (Verify EEPROM stores the new bed PID values via M503.)

recommendations.title: Junction Deviation value — 0.08 is reasonable; verify and lower if ringing persists
recommendations.recommendedValue: Keep 0.08 as start; consider 0.04-0.05 if you see corner ghosting (or after enabling Input Shaping)
recommendations.rationale: 0.08 mm is exactly the value derived from the classic Ender 3 jerk equivalence: JD = 0.4 * jerk^2 / accel = 0.4 * 10^2 / 500 = 0.08, so it is a sensible default and consistent with the printer's 500 mm/s^2 accel. It is on the aggressive side for a direct drive (heavier moving mass on X), so if you see ringing at corners, dropping toward 0.04-0.05 tightens cornering. Note S_CURVE must be off (recommended above) for JD to behave correctly. Once Input Shaping handles ringing, you can keep JD higher for speed.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration.h: #define JUNCTION_DEVIATION_MM 0.08 (current). To experiment live without reflashing: M205 J0.05 then M500. Tune empirically with a JD calibration cube.

recommendations.title: Increase ABL grid density (and adopt high-speed probing tuning)
recommendations.recommendedValue: 5x5 (optionally consider UBL); keep bilinear if happy
recommendations.rationale: On a 210x210 probing area a 4x4 grid samples every ~70 mm. A glass/PEI Ender bed is usually smooth enough that 4x4 is adequate, but 5x5 better captures center bow/corners with minimal added probe time and improves first-layer flatness. The F103 handles 5x5 bilinear easily. Diminishing returns beyond 5x5 for bilinear; if you want true mesh interpolation with manual touch-ups, UBL is the upgrade path (more flash/RAM, more complex). Pair with MULTIPLE_PROBING already at 2 for repeatability.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration.h (bilinear block): #define GRID_MAX_POINTS_X 5 (Y follows X). Reflash, re-run G29 and M500. Optional: switch to AUTO_BED_LEVELING_UBL for finer mesh + editable points.

recommendations.title: Sensorless homing on TMC2209 — viable but not recommended over the working CRTouch/endstops
recommendations.currentValue: SENSORLESS_HOMING and SENSORLESS_PROBING disabled; STEALTHCHOP_XY enabled; physical endstops in use
recommendations.recommendedValue: Leave disabled unless removing endstops is a goal; if enabled, X/Y only (never Z)
recommendations.rationale: TMC2209 StallGuard4 sensorless homing works on X/Y and can be reliable once tuned, but it requires StealthChop active during homing, careful SGTHRD/homing-current tuning, a homing back-off, and is sensitive to belt tension, speed, and temperature. It removes only the X/Y endstops (this printer already has working endstops), so the upside is marginal. Z sensorless homing is explicitly not recommended (poor first-layer accuracy, nozzle/bed crash risk) — and this printer correctly uses the CRTouch for Z. Net: not worth the reliability tradeoff here unless you specifically want to delete endstop hardware.
recommendations.howToApply: If pursued: Configuration_adv.h enable #define SENSORLESS_HOMING, set X/Y STALL_SENSITIVITY, enable SENSORLESS_BACKOFF_MM { 2, 2, 0 }, keep STEALTHCHOP_XY on, tune with M914 X.. Y.. then M500. Keep Z on CRTouch (do NOT enable SENSORLESS Z).

recommendations.title: S-curve acceleration interactions — must be off (covered above), summary of why
recommendations.rationale: Consolidated rationale: S_CURVE conflicts with all three of the other motion features this printer uses or should use — (a) LIN_ADVANCE (buggy timing, gated behind EXPERIMENTAL_SCURVE sanity check), (b) JUNCTION_DEVIATION (S-curve silently stops working without CLASSIC_JERK), and (c) INPUT_SHAPING (mutually exclusive). There is no configuration in which keeping S_CURVE on is correct for this build. Disabling it is the single change that unblocks LIN_ADVANCE tuning, clean Junction Deviation behavior, and Input Shaping.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration.h: //#define S_CURVE_ACCELERATION . (Same action as the dedicated S_CURVE recommendation; listed here for the S-curve-interactions topic.)

recommendations.title: Probe / leveling refinements (CRTouch in BLTOUCH mode)
recommendations.currentValue: Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_FAST 240, SLOW 120 mm/min; MULTIPLE_PROBING 2; PROBING_MARGIN 50; offset {-30,-40,-3.49}; preheat-before-leveling on; RESTORE_LEVELING_AFTER_G28 on; babystep Z-offset DISABLED
recommendations.recommendedValue: Enable BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET; keep SLOW ~120 (raise to <=180 only if accurate); verify offset with M48; consider tightening PROBING_MARGIN
recommendations.rationale: CR-Touch is sensitive to high-speed timing; a slow final probe (SLOW 120 mm/min) is exactly right for repeatability, and MULTIPLE_PROBING 2 averages out noise — both good. The most useful refinement: enable BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET so live-Z babystepping persists into the Z probe offset (saved with M500), letting you dial first-layer squish on the fly — currently babystepping is on but it doesn't adjust the probe offset, so tweaks are lost. PROBING_MARGIN 50 is conservative given the firmware bed was already reduced to 210x210 to keep the probe on-bed; you may be able to lower the margin to widen the leveled area (validate the -30/-40 nozzle-to-probe offset keeps all points on the physical bed). Run M48 to confirm probe repeatability (<0.01-0.02 mm is good) before trusting the mesh.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration.h: enable #define BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET (under BABYSTEPPING). Validate probe: M48 P10. Re-check Z offset: M851 Z-3.49 then live-adjust with babystep and M500. Optionally reduce #define PROBING_MARGIN after confirming reachable points. Consider BLTOUCH_DELAY tuning only if probing errors appear.

recommendations.title: EDITABLE_STEPS_PER_UNIT — keep enabled
recommendations.rationale: This option keeps steps/mm editable at runtime (M92) and stored in EEPROM. Its flash/RAM cost is negligible on the STM32F103 (256 KB flash, plenty of headroom for this feature set). Disabling it has a documented downside: it can trigger EEPROM-validation/'EEPROM corrupt' messages on boot. Since the extruder steps are calibrated (E=423 for the Sprite) and you may re-calibrate per future mods, keeping M92 editable is convenient. No reason to disable on this board.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration.h: leave #define EDITABLE_STEPS_PER_UNIT enabled. Use M92 E423 (and M500) for runtime steps changes. Only disable if you ever hit a genuine flash-space wall, which this config is far from.

recommendations.title: Thermal protection — keep enabled; relax WATCH periods only until PID is tuned
recommendations.currentValue: THERMAL_PROTECTION for hotend/bed/chamber ENABLED; hotend PID set; bed currently bang-bang (PID being added)
recommendations.recommendedValue: Keep all thermal protection ON; temporarily relax WATCH/HYSTERESIS during PID autotune, then restore defaults
recommendations.rationale: Thermal protection is a safety feature and must stay enabled — never disable it to silence errors. The only legitimate tweak: while running bed PID autotune (newly enabled) or if you get false 'heating failed' trips, temporarily lengthen WATCH_TEMP_PERIOD / WATCH_BED_TEMP_PERIOD (e.g. 40-60 s) and increase WATCH_*_HYSTERESIS so an untuned PID doesn't false-trigger; then return to defaults (default ~ 40 s / 4 C drift window) once tuned. The Sprite Pro heats fast, so the default 20 s/2 C watch window for the hotend is fine and no relaxation is normally needed. M303 PID tuning (hotend + bed) is the real fix for any thermal-protection false positives.
recommendations.howToApply: Configuration.h / Configuration_adv.h: keep THERMAL_PROTECTION_HOTENDS / _BED / _CHAMBER defined. If false trips during tuning: raise WATCH_BED_TEMP_PERIOD to 60 and WATCH_BED_TEMP_INCREASE / hysteresis slightly, reflash, autotune (M303), then revert. Always run M303 E0 (hotend) and M303 E-1 (bed) first.

procedure: SUGGESTED TUNING ORDER (each step depends on the previous): 1) Reflash with S_CURVE_ACCELERATION disabled and PIDTEMPBED enabled. 2) PID autotune hotend (M303 E0 S210 C8 U1) and bed (M303 E-1 S60 C8 U1), M500. 3) Verify Junction Deviation 0.08 with a cornering test; lower toward 0.05 if ringing. 4) Enable + tune Input Shaping: print 15-60 Hz ringing tower at high accel, read cleanest band, M593 X F.. Y F.., M500. 5) ONLY THEN tune LIN_ADVANCE: M900 K sweep starting near 0.04 on a single-wall test, pick uniform line width, set ADVANCE_K and M500. Pass/fail: bed temp should hold within ~+/-0.5 C after PID (vs +/-1-3 C bang-bang); corners should be sharp with no ghosting after IS; line width uniform with no corner blobs/gaps after LA. M48 probe repeatability should be <0.02 mm.
imageNeeds: LIN_ADVANCE / pressure-advance calibration pattern showing under-tuned (corner blobs) vs over-tuned (corner gaps) vs correct uniform line width
Input Shaping ringing tower with frequency sweep labeled, showing where ghosting disappears
Diagram of SKR Mini E3 V2.0 driver layout (X/Y/Z/E only, no 5th driver) illustrating why independent dual-Z is impossible and how a 2nd Z motor wires in series to the single Z driver
Bed temperature graph: bang-bang oscillation vs PID stability
ABL 4x4 vs 5x5 probe grid overlay on a 210x210 bed showing coverage difference
